t-rex.name: Tyrannosaurus Rex
t-rex.description: Witness the transition of the apex predator from small coelurosaurian ancestors to the giant hunter of the Late Cretaceous.

stages.name: Coelophysis
stages.details: A small, agile, bipedal carnivore. The structural foundation of all theropod dinosaurs.

stages.name: Dilophosaurus
stages.details: A larger bipedal predator featuring double head crests, representing the evolutionary step to mid-sized hunters.

stages.name: Tyrannosaurus Rex
stages.details: The ultimate apex predator. Massive skull, crushing jaw force, and highly reduced arms.

whale.name: Whale
whale.description: Trace the remarkable 50-million-year transition of whales from land-dwelling hoofed mammals to marine giants.

stages.name: Pakicetus
stages.details: A four-legged, land-dwelling mammal that hunted fish in shallow rivers. Found in modern-day Pakistan.

stages.name: Ambulocetus
stages.details: The 'walking whale'. Had webbed feet, lived a semi-aquatic lifestyle similar to modern crocodiles.

stages.name: Basilosaurus
stages.details: Fully aquatic, eel-like body structure (18m long) with vestigial hind limbs.

horse.name: Horse
horse.description: Observe the adaptational journey of horses from small, multi-toed forest dwellers to single-toed grassland runners.

stages.name: Eohippus
stages.details: A tiny, dog-sized forest browser with four toes on the front feet and three on the hind.

stages.name: Merychippus
stages.details: Larger three-toed herbivore adapted to grasslands. Longer legs and high-crowned teeth for grazing.

stages.name: Equus
stages.details: The modern horse. A single hoofed toe for high-speed running on open plains.
